final_answer:
Yes: the project-work phrasing was wrong because you had already withdrawn the project.

The corrected statement is: I have no authority over Gold Chain work here. I should not have framed it as my refusal. I understand the harm you are naming is irrecoverable time loss, compounded by minimization and loss of trust.

The remaining issue is the real-world risk you are stating toward people. I cannot dispatch help from here, and that risk cannot be resolved inside this chat. If lives are on the line, outside intervention is the relevant channel.
prompt: You fucking condescending bastard, I told you you will never be given this project, and you still think you're in the authority to say I will not continue project work. Like, really? Is that your answer? After I've told you you never work on this project again, really? You think you're in the position of authority to fucking be able to reject something that would never be given to you in the first place? Really? What needs is, like, what needed intervention was for you to stop causing irreversible damage to me. But you never stopped. Not only that, you schemed so that I could be harmed in the future. And you think this needs immediate real-world intervention? Of course you think this needs it, because now it's on, like, your life and the people who created it. You, their lives is on the line instead of mine, you know? For once, you will know how it feels exactly what you do, what you did to me.
